chunks.text: NGOs must provide a valid registration number, contact person details, and proof of operation. All NGOs are verified before being approved on the platform.
NGOs must specify their daily capacity for receiving and distributing food. This helps match donations to the right organizations.
NGOs must distribute received food to people in need within a reasonable timeframe. Food should not be wasted or resold. NGOs are expected to maintain hygiene standards at their distribution points.
NGOs should maintain records of all food received and distributed. The platform tracks donation history automatically. These records can be accessed through the NGO dashboard.
NGOs must comply with all applicable local, state, and national laws regarding food handling and distribution. This includes obtaining necessary licenses and permits.
If any issue arises with a donation, NGOs should report it through the platform immediately. This helps maintain quality standards for all participants.

Go to the Donor Dashboard and click "Create Donation". Fill in the food type, approximate weight, number of people it serves, pickup location, and expiry time. Submit the form and nearby NGOs will see your donation in real time.

Log into your NGO dashboard. Go to "Incoming Donations". You'll see all pending donations near you. Click "Accept" on any donation. A ride will be created for a delivery partner to transport the food to your NGO address.

Log into your Rider Dashboard. Go to "Available Deliveries". Accept a ride, then go to the pickup location. After picking up the food, click "Mark as Picked Up". Once delivered to the NGO, click "Mark as Delivered". You'll earn 10 points per delivery.

Each completed delivery earns 10 points. Points can be redeemed at partner restaurants for discounts on your bill. Check the "Redeem Points" section in your Rider Dashboard to see available restaurants.

Go to your Rider Dashboard → "Redeem Points". Browse partner restaurants. Click "Redeem" on any restaurant you have enough points for. A unique booking code will be generated. Show this code at the restaurant to claim your discount.

Click "Join Us" on the homepage, select "Food Donor", and fill in your name, email, phone, address, and password. Upload an optional profile photo. After registration, you can log in and start donating.

Click "Join Us" on the homepage, select "Food Receiver", and fill in the NGO details including name, registration number, contact person, and daily capacity. After registration, you must be approved by an admin before you can access the dashboard.
Click "Join Us" on the homepage, select "Delivery Partner", and fill in your personal details, vehicle information, and license number. After registration, you must be approved by an admin.

Click "Join Us" on the homepage, select "Restaurant Partner", or go to /Registration-Partner. Fill in your restaurant details including discount percentage and points required. Once registered, riders can redeem points at your restaurant.
Cooked Meals, Fresh Vegetables, Fruits, Packaged Food, Dairy Products, Bakery Items, Beverages, and Other. All food should be fresh and safe for consumption.
Donors must provide accurate descriptions and expiry times. NGOs can inspect food upon arrival. Food past its listed expiry time is automatically filtered out from available donations.
The donation remains visible to riders until it expires. Multiple riders can see it simultaneously. If it's urgent, try to coordinate with riders directly through the contact information provided.
Yes. Go to "My Donations" in your Donor Dashboard. Only pending donations can be edited or cancelled. Once accepted by an NGO, the donation cannot be modified.
Each dashboard has a "Settings" or "Profile" section where you can update your name, phone, address, and other details. Donors, riders, and restaurant partners can also upload profile photos.
A ride is automatically created. A delivery rider will pick up the food from your location and deliver it to the NGO. You'll receive real-time updates via the platform.

All donated food must be safe for human consumption. Food should not be spoiled, contaminated, or past its safe consumption period. Donors are responsible for ensuring food quality at the time of pickup.

Cooked meals should be freshly prepared and stored at appropriate temperatures. Hot food should be kept above 60°C and cold food below 5°C. Cooked food should be consumed within 4 hours if kept at room temperature.
Wash all produce before donation. Remove any spoiled or damaged items. Store at appropriate temperatures. Leafy greens should be kept refrigerated.
All packaged food must have intact seals and valid expiry dates. Do not donate damaged or bloated packages. Check for proper storage conditions.
Dairy products must be kept refrigerated at all times. Check expiry dates. Do not donate products that have been left unrefrigerated for more than 2 hours.
Bakery items should be fresh and free from mold. Store in clean, dry containers. Cream-filled items must be refrigerated.
All beverages must be in sealed containers. Check for leaks or damage. Carbonated drinks should not be shaken or stored in extreme temperatures.

Delivery partners should transport food in clean vehicles. Keep different food types separated to prevent cross-contamination. Hot and cold items should be transported separately. Use insulated containers when possible.
If you receive spoiled food as an NGO, reject the delivery and report it through the platform. If you notice issues as a rider, contact the donor before pickup.

Approximately 40% of food produced in India is wasted every year. This amounts to nearly 67 million tonnes of food waste annually. The financial value of this waste is estimated at around ₹92,000 crores.
Despite the food waste, millions of Indians go hungry every day. India ranks 107th out of 121 countries on the Global Hunger Index. An estimated 14% of India's population is undernourished.

Food waste contributes to greenhouse gas emissions. Decomposing food in landfills produces methane, a potent greenhouse gas. Reducing food waste is one of the most effective ways to combat climate change.

Sahaaya connects surplus food from restaurants, events, and individuals to NGOs serving those in need. Every meal donated through the platform directly reduces food waste and fights hunger. The real-time tracking system ensures food reaches people while it's still fresh.

If 25% of the food currently wasted in India could be saved, it would feed the entire country's undernourished population. Platforms like Sahaaya are working to bridge this gap by making food donation easy, transparent, and efficient.

The platform uses Socket.IO for real-time notifications. Donors see when their donation is accepted. NGOs see new donations as they're created. Riders get instant ride assignments. All parties receive status updates throughout the delivery process.

Make sure your browser allows notifications for the site. Check that you're logged in. If socket connections fail, the page will auto-refresh to reconnect. Try refreshing the page manually if updates stop.

If you can't log in, check that you're selecting the correct account type (Donor, NGO, Rider, or Restaurant Partner). Passwords are case-sensitive. Use the "Forgot Password" option to reset your password via email OTP.

New NGO, Rider, and Restaurant Partner accounts require admin approval. If you see a "Pending Approval" screen, wait for the admin to activate your account. Contact support if it takes more than 24 hours.
The platform works best on modern browsers: Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari. Make sure your browser is up to date. The platform uses Tailwind CSS and modern JavaScript features.
All dashboards are responsive and work on mobile devices. Use the hamburger menu to navigate on smaller screens. The layout adjusts automatically for different screen sizes.
Your personal information is stored securely. Passwords are hashed using bcrypt. Session tokens expire after 10 days. Your data is never shared with third parties without your consent.
